items.title: India-UK trade pact comes into force, gold standard of FTA for both
items.summary: India and the UK have operationalized a free trade agreement, enhancing economic ties. This pact allows zero duty entry for many Indian goods into the British market. Bilateral trade aims to reach $100 billion by 2030, increasing from $60 billion. The agreement supports Indian farmers, entrepreneurs, and MSMEs with stronger market access. It also deepens cooperation in technology, services, and skilled talent mobility.

items.title: Pentair shares dip 17% on dour forecast amid weak pool sales

High interest rates and inflation have pressured spending on outdoor projects, while distributors continue inventory cuts. The outlook signals a deeper slowdown in the pool equipment industry and broader demand concerns.

items.title: Fed's Cook says she is prepared to act soon if inflation does not begin to slow
items.summary: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook signalled readiness to act if inflation fails to ease, while allowing more time to assess trends. She flagged rising risks from AI investment, tariffs and geopolitical tensions. With inflation still above target, markets are increasingly factoring in the possibility of a rate hike later this year.

Furthermore, foreign portfolio investors began to temper their divestments in the local markets, with the central bank noting an outflow in foreign direct investment.

This provision may offer a cheaper compliance option than statutory penalties. The company also requested that earned credits should be allowed to carry forward.

items.title: India bans import of goods made with forced labour
items.summary: India has amended its foreign trade policy to ban goods produced using forced labour. This move comes amid a US investigation into forced labour practices globally. The new policy prohibits imports of goods manufactured wholly or in part through forced labour. This prohibition will take effect after a 30-day period from its official gazette publication. The government can issue notifications to ban specific goods based on evidence of forced labour.

items.title: Government may let statutory bodies to regulate members in multidisciplinary partnerships

This approach would enable existing watchdogs to oversee chartered accountants and company secretaries. Such a framework aims to facilitate the creation of larger Indian professional services firms. These firms could then better compete with global giants in the audit market.

items.title: June vegetable oil imports dip 29 per cent
items.summary: The decline was primarily driven by a sharp fall in palm oil imports, which dropped 10.5% from the previous month as the price gap between palm oil and other edible oils narrowed significantly. Soyabean oil imports also fell 23%. A sharp 18-20% year-on-year increase in international prices of major cooking oils, driven by their diversion for biofuel production and an 11% depreciation of the Indian rupee also weighed on imports.

items.title: India-UK FTA comes into effect today, unlocking duty-free access for Indian exports
items.summary: India and the UK have enacted a free trade agreement, opening significant export opportunities. This pact offers duty-free access on ninety-nine percent of India's tariff lines to the UK. Indian manufacturers will gain preferential entry into the UK's sixth-largest economy. The agreement also provides regulatory certainty for Indian service providers across various sectors. However, experts caution that quality standards and non-tariff barriers remain crucial for export success.
